_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING** — Track EPA endangerment-finding rescission and Phase 3 reconsideration proceedings actively. In February 2026, EPA finalized repeal of the 2009 GHG endangerment finding — the legal underpinning of the Phase 3 rule — and formally announced reconsideration of the Phase 3 standards in March 2025. The enforcement status of MY 2027-onwards standards is now legally uncertain and subject to ongoing litigation from both environmental groups and industry. The workspace must assign a designated regulatory monitor to track EPA rulemaking docket EPA-HQ-OAR-2022-0985 for any formal rescission notices, replacement rule proposals, or court injunctions that alter fleet-procurement obligations. No fixed deadline — ongoing.

- **COMPETITIVE EDGE** — Audit the road-freight subcontractor and carrier panel now to identify which carriers operate MY 2027+ compliant or ZEV-capable fleets. Manufacturers have already invested in next-generation heavy-duty vehicle technology that will reach market regardless of Phase 3 enforcement status; MY 2027+ vehicles will incorporate advanced emissions architectures. Carriers aligned with these platforms present lower regulatory and reputational risk for the workspace's high-value cargo verticals (live events, fine art, luxury, film/TV, humanitarian). No hard federal deadline today, but carrier refresh cycles make this a rolling 30-day action.

- **MONITORING** — Assess California Advanced Clean Fleets (ACF) and Advanced Clean Trucks (ACT) exposure for any workspace carriers operating California routes (air-cargo drayage, port-to-warehouse legs on Americas lanes). Congress used the Congressional Review Act to disapprove EPA's waiver for the ACT rule; California withdrew the ACF waiver request in January 2025. State-level obligations on California-route carriers may therefore differ materially from federal Phase 3 status. Legal clarity is expected following a U.S. Supreme Court oral argument scheduled for fall 2025. Deadline: obtain carrier compliance declarations by end of 30-day window.

- **MONITORING** — Review customer sustainability reporting obligations (e.g., SEC climate disclosure, EU CSRD scope-3 Scope 3 reporting for EU clients) that rely on carrier GHG certification under Phase 3. Even if federal enforcement of Phase 3 is ultimately rescinded, large shipper clients in the workspace's luxury, fine-art, and humanitarian verticals may contractually require certified low-emission road transport. Identify affected client contracts within 30 days.

*Source: Greenhouse Gas Emissions Standards for Heavy-Duty Vehicles—Phase 3, EPA, Federal Register Vol. 89, April 22, 2024. https://federalregister.gov/documents/2024/04/22/2024-06809/greenhouse-gas-emissions-standards-for-heavy-duty-vehicles-phase-3. Ballotpedia, GHG Phase 3 rule (2024), accessed May 2025. https://ballotpedia.org/Greenhouse_Gas_Emissions_Standards_for_Heavy-Duty_Vehicles-Phase_3_rule_(2024). Heavy Vehicle Inspection, EPA Phase 3 GHG Standards Guide 2027, March 2026. https://heavyvehicleinspection.com/blog/post/epa-phase-3-ghg-standards.*
